_comment: Container config for the Baget agent group. Mounts the baget-mcp tools (built into this fork's container/agent-runner) and threads the per-(user, company) channel token + company id from OneCLI / agent-group settings. Adjust BAGET_API_BASE_URL via /customize for staging vs prod.
_agent_provider_note: Use OpenCode (not the Anthropic Agent SDK default) so the channel agent runs on Vertex Gemini Flash — same model the dashboard worker uses, ~10× cheaper than Claude Sonnet for this workload, and matches the prompt-tuning the team-of-six persona is calibrated against. Requires `/add-opencode` to have been run on this nanoclaw install (the skill copies the OpenCode provider files in from the upstream `providers` branch). See BAGET-DEPLOY.md "Provider config" for the host env vars.

env._BAGET_CHANNEL_TOKEN_note: Injected by OneCLI from the agent vault (credential: baget-channel-token). Each agent has its own scoped token tied to (user_id, company_id, conversation_id). Revoking from the dashboard immediately invalidates writes.
env._BAGET_COMPANY_ID_note: Set per-agent-group from the founder's selected company at pairing time. Stored in agent_groups.container_config and re-injected on every spawn.
env._OPENCODE_PROVIDER_note: Set on the HOST (not in container_config). When `agent_provider=opencode`, the host injects OPENCODE_PROVIDER + OPENCODE_MODEL + OPENCODE_SMALL_MODEL into the spawned container. For Baget: OPENCODE_PROVIDER=google, OPENCODE_MODEL=google/gemini-2.5-flash (matches the dashboard worker's stack).

secrets: _google-genai-api-key_note: register the Vertex/Google API key in OneCLI with --host-pattern=generativelanguage.googleapis.com so requests proxy through OneCLI without the key ever entering env. Then `onecli agents set-secrets --id <agent-id> --secret-ids <id>` to grant this agent access (selective mode default).

mcp_servers.name: baget
mcp_servers._comment: baget-mcp lives in container/agent-runner/src/mcp-tools/baget.ts and is registered automatically by the barrel import. No separate MCP server process — the same container/agent-runner stdio transport carries baget tools alongside core/scheduling/interactive/agents.
